problem: What is the value of $b$ if $-x^2+bx-5<0$ only when $x\in (-\infty, 1)\cup(5,\infty)$?

classified_questions.question: What is the relationship between the values of $b$ and the inequality $-x^2 + bx - 5 < 0$ only holding true when $x$ is in the interval $(-\infty, 1)\cup(5,\infty)$?
classified_questions.label: b
